Sport|Olympics

Great Britainare tipped to finish theParis Olympicsin fourth place in themedalstable.

Gracenote, which predicts the medal tally and final positions for each Games, is forecasting a total of 63 medals forTeam GB.

In its model, that would put the British with the third-highest medal count behind the United States and China, but pushed into fourth by hosts France by virtue of their higher predicted gold medal tally (27 to 17).

The anticipated haul is largely similar to the last Games in Tokyo, whereTeam GB ended fourth in the tablewith 64 medals, 22 of which were gold.

In its report, Gracenote wrote: “Great Britain can sustain its winning performance of 60 or more medals for a fourth successive Games.

“British success over the past three summer Olympics has been built on having medal winners in at least 20 sports.

“Gracenote’s final virtual medal table has winners in 21 different sports, suggesting this trend is set to continue.

“Prior to the 2012 Olympics, Great Britain had not won medals in at least 20 sports for over 100 years, since hosting [the Games] in 1908.”

title: Olympics: Team GB medal haul prediction revealed as Paris Games approach | The Standard
author: Matt Majendie
description: Great Britain are expected to achieve similar results to Tokyo, where they matched their total from London 2012
keywords: Paris Olympics, Team GB, Great Britain, medals
